tweets.title: BREAKING: Pakistan Threatens India With Military Action Over Indus Waters Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif has claimed that Islamabad could resort to war if its water security is threatened, linking the issue directly to national security amid the ongoing dispute over the Indus Waters Treaty. “The moment we feel that our national security, and water is part of our national security, is being threatened, we will go to war against India. Definitely,” he said. The remarks come more than a year after India suspended the treaty following the Pahalgam terror attack, a move that has intensified pressure on Pakistan's already worsening water situation.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Pakistan Threatens India With Military Action Over Indus Waters

Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif has claimed that Islamabad could resort to war if its water security is threatened, linking the issue directly to national security amid the ongoing dispute over the Indus Waters Treaty.

“The moment we feel that our national security, and water is part of our national security, is being threatened, we will go to war against India. Definitely,” he said.

The remarks come more than a year after India suspended the treaty following the Pahalgam terror attack, a move that has intensified pressure on Pakistan's already worsening water situation.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: A country, company, or organization that has the capability to successfully reverse engineer a complex system and manufacture it at scale is often much closer to developing its own indigenous version than many people realize. Reverse engineering is not magic; it requires deep expertise in materials, design, testing, manufacturing, and quality control. Once you have reached that level, creating an original product becomes a matter of time, investment, and intent. The real challenge is building the capability and the determination to do it. Those who lack that capability often come up with reasons why it cannot be done.
tweets.content:
A country, company, or organization that has the capability to successfully reverse engineer a complex system and manufacture it at scale is often much closer to developing its own indigenous version than many people realize.

Reverse engineering is not magic; it requires deep expertise in materials, design, testing, manufacturing, and quality control. Once you have reached that level, creating an original product becomes a matter of time, investment, and intent.

The real challenge is building the capability and the determination to do it. Those who lack that capability often come up with reasons why it cannot be done.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: RT by @sidhant: (1/2) Marking #InternationalDayofYoga with enthusiasm across the fast growing cities of #Yiwu, #Keqiao, #Kunshan, #Taizhou and #Nanjing in Yangtze River Delta region of Eastern China, the vibrant Indian Diaspora came together with Yoga practitioners amongst the local communities for an unprecedented scale of yoga, meditation and heath awareness training campaigns and events. ➡️ On the auspicious occasion of #DragonBoatFestival, thousands of Yoga enthusiasts participated in the calming international tradition for the International Day of Yoga, getting into alignment from #Yiwu to #Keqiao, from #Kunshan to #Taizhou to #Nanjing, regardless of weather conditions. 🕉️🧘🌧️🌞 ➡️ The celebrations this year once again brought home that since the inception of IDY day in 2015 by @UN, its popularity has grown worldwide and exploded in places like China. 🔥💫 ➡️ Yoga embodies timeless values of mindfulness, balance, and harmony with nature -- values that continue to resonate deeply with people in both India and China. 🧘‍♂️🌎🙏🏼 #IDY2026 #YogaForHealthyAgeing CG @PratikMathur1 @MEAIndia @IndianDiplomacy @moayush @diaspora_india @EOIBeijing
tweets.content:
(1/2)

Marking #InternationalDayofYoga with enthusiasm across the fast growing cities of #Yiwu, #Keqiao, #Kunshan, #Taizhou and #Nanjing in Yangtze River Delta region of Eastern China, the vibrant Indian Diaspora came together with Yoga practitioners amongst the local communities for an unprecedented scale of yoga, meditation and heath awareness training campaigns and events.

➡️ On the auspicious occasion of #DragonBoatFestival, thousands of Yoga enthusiasts participated in the calming international tradition for the International Day of Yoga, getting into alignment from #Yiwu to #Keqiao, from #Kunshan to #Taizhou to #Nanjing, regardless of weather conditions. 🕉️🧘🌧️🌞

➡️ The celebrations this year once again brought home that since the inception of IDY day in 2015 by @UN, its popularity has grown worldwide and exploded in places like China. 🔥💫

➡️ Yoga embodies timeless values of mindfulness, balance, and harmony with nature -- values that continue to resonate deeply with people in both India and China. 🧘‍♂️🌎🙏🏼

#IDY2026 #YogaForHealthyAgeing

CG @PratikMathur1 @MEAIndia @IndianDiplomacy @moayush @diaspora_india @EOIBeijing


Video

tweets.title: The €100-billion Future Combat Air System (FCAS/SCAF) program has collapsed following the decision by Germany and France to abandon the joint development of the core New Generation Fighter. Irreconcilable industrial disputes between Dassault Aviation and Airbus, combined with diverging military requirements for the aircraft, led to the project's failure.
tweets.content:
The €100-billion Future Combat Air System (FCAS/SCAF) program has collapsed following the decision by Germany and France to abandon the joint development of the core New Generation Fighter.

Irreconcilable industrial disputes between Dassault Aviation and Airbus, combined with diverging military requirements for the aircraft, led to the project's failure.

tweets.title: Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 21st Jun 2026: - We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East - ⁠Follow us for daily Brief ● US VP Vance arrived at Bürgenstock, Switzerland, for direct talks with an Iranian delegation led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Abbas Araghchi. ● Following Iran’s announcement closing the Strait, no vessels have traversed the waterway, despite contradicting US claims. ● Three Chinese-linked oil tankers - ELVA, VIRGO, and VIGOR - laden with Iranian oil are heading toward the Strait of Hormuz. ● Pakistan's FM Ishaq Dar stated that an agreement has been reached between the US and Iran to lower the enrichment level of Tehran’s highly enriched uranium stockpile. ● A product tanker was approached by a skiff with five armed individuals attempting to board, 50 nautical miles southeast of Yemen. END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
21st Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● US VP Vance arrived at Bürgenstock, Switzerland, for direct talks with an Iranian delegation led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Abbas Araghchi.

● Following Iran’s announcement closing the Strait, no vessels have traversed the waterway, despite contradicting US claims.

● Three Chinese-linked oil tankers - ELVA, VIRGO, and VIGOR - laden with Iranian oil are heading toward the Strait of Hormuz.

● Pakistan's FM Ishaq Dar stated that an agreement has been reached between the US and Iran to lower the enrichment level of Tehran’s highly enriched uranium stockpile.

● A product tanker was approached by a skiff with five armed individuals attempting to board, 50 nautical miles southeast of Yemen.

END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: If you look at the bigger picture, a successful outcome from the Iran–US talks and the reopening of Hormuz would be a net positive for India. I understand why some are focused on Pakistan’s attempts to market itself as a “peace broker.” The reality, however, is that Pakistan will still have to go knocking on the doors of the IMF, the Gulf states, the US, or China for economic support. If the global environment becomes conducive to business again, it benefits not just India, but also China, Europe, and ASEAN economies over the longer term. & for that if you have to give this guy Nobel give it.
tweets.content:
If you look at the bigger picture, a successful outcome from the Iran–US talks and the reopening of Hormuz would be a net positive for India.

I understand why some are focused on Pakistan’s attempts to market itself as a “peace broker.” The reality, however, is that Pakistan will still have to go knocking on the doors of the IMF, the Gulf states, the US, or China for economic support.

If the global environment becomes conducive to business again, it benefits not just India, but also China, Europe, and ASEAN economies over the longer term.

& for that if you have to give this guy Nobel give it.

tweets.title: Indian media is conflating two separate stories. India’s interest has been in Rafale F4-class capability and near-term force expansion, not waiting for Rafale F5. Meanwhile, the Rafale F5 story is fundamentally about France finding funding, industrial partners and launch customers after the UAE reportedly stepped back over technology-transfer disagreements. Until Paris and Abu Dhabi reach a new understanding, the much-discussed “Super Rafale” remains more of a roadmap than a fielded reality.
tweets.content:
Indian media is conflating two separate stories. India’s interest has been in Rafale F4-class capability and near-term force expansion, not waiting for Rafale F5.

Meanwhile, the Rafale F5 story is fundamentally about France finding funding, industrial partners and launch customers after the UAE reportedly stepped back over technology-transfer disagreements. Until Paris and Abu Dhabi reach a new understanding, the much-discussed “Super Rafale” remains more of a roadmap than a fielded reality.

tweets.content:
Pakistani delegation at the Lake Lucerne Summit:

1. Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif – Prime Minister
2. Field Marshal Asim Munir – Chief of Defence Forces
3. Mohsin Naqvi – Interior Minister
4. Major General Kashif Abdullah – DG (Military Operations)
5. Major General Syed Jawad Tariq – Private Secretary to the CAOS

Looks less like a government delegation and more like Asim Munir’s personal entourage. 😂

Interesting that Shehbaz’s usual political heavyweights, Ishaq Dar and Attaullah Tarar, are nowhere to be seen. Seems the civilians got invited to the photo-op, while the real delegation came from GHQ. 😏

tweets.title: Whether exiting the MTA program was a mistake cannot be judged in isolation. One first needs to understand why a significant portion of the IAF’s IL-76 fleet is parked, with nearly 17 aircraft are partially deteriorating (nor all). If sustainment, availability, and lifecycle costs of the existing heavy-lift fleet influenced the IAF’s thinking, then the decision to walk away from MTA may have appeared more rational at the time than it does in hindsight. However walking out and not restarting other program right away was a mistake indeed.
tweets.content:
Whether exiting the MTA program was a mistake cannot be judged in isolation.

One first needs to understand why a significant portion of the IAF’s IL-76 fleet is parked, with nearly 17 aircraft are partially deteriorating (nor all). If sustainment, availability, and lifecycle costs of the existing heavy-lift fleet influenced the IAF’s thinking, then the decision to walk away from MTA may have appeared more rational at the time than it does in hindsight.

However walking out and not restarting other program right away was a mistake indeed.

tweets.title: The Indian Army is set to receive indigenously manufactured loitering munitions under a partnership between Indian defence firm SMPP and European defence group KNDS. The agreement was signed at the Eurosatory Exhibition in Paris on 18 June. 🔹 The systems include the VELOCE and RODEUR loitering munitions, along with the ISTAR drone family. 🔹 Designed for "search-and-destroy" missions, they can locate, track, and engage high-value targets with precision. 🔹 The munitions feature hybrid GNSS-INS guidance and fire-and-forget capability, enhancing effectiveness in complex battlefield environments. 🔹 The programme aligns with the Make in India and Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiatives, with a focus on domestic production.
tweets.content:
The Indian Army is set to receive indigenously manufactured loitering munitions under a partnership between Indian defence firm SMPP and European defence group KNDS. The agreement was signed at the Eurosatory Exhibition in Paris on 18 June.

🔹 The systems include the VELOCE and RODEUR loitering munitions, along with the ISTAR drone family.
🔹 Designed for "search-and-destroy" missions, they can locate, track, and engage high-value targets with precision.
🔹 The munitions feature hybrid GNSS-INS guidance and fire-and-forget capability, enhancing effectiveness in complex battlefield environments.
🔹 The programme aligns with the Make in India and Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiatives, with a focus on domestic production.

tweets.title: Russia has reportedly begun operational deployment of the Volna Kupol Garant electronic warfare system, designed to disrupt Starlink satellite communications used for drone control, reconnaissance, and battlefield coordination. According to reports, the system jams signals in the 14–14.5 GHz band by transmitting powerful interference toward Starlink satellites. One system is said to cover an area of roughly 20 sq km and is intended to protect high-value assets such as military bases, bridges, and energy infrastructure. Russian authorities have not officially commented on the reported deployment.
tweets.content:
Russia has reportedly begun operational deployment of the Volna Kupol Garant electronic warfare system, designed to disrupt Starlink satellite communications used for drone control, reconnaissance, and battlefield coordination.

According to reports, the system jams signals in the 14–14.5 GHz band by transmitting powerful interference toward Starlink satellites. One system is said to cover an area of roughly 20 sq km and is intended to protect high-value assets such as military bases, bridges, and energy infrastructure.

Russian authorities have not officially commented on the reported deployment.

tweets.title: This is how Su30 fleet was created over the years : 📅 30 Nov 1996 – 40 aircraft (Russia) 📅 18 Dec 1998 – 10 aircraft (Russia) 📅 28 Dec 2000 – 140 aircraft (HAL) 📅 10 Oct 2007 – 40 aircraft (HAL) 📅 24 Dec 2012 – 42 aircraft (HAL) After that, there was a gap of over a decade. 📅 15 Sep 2023 – AoN for 12 replacement aircraft 📅 12 Dec 2024 – ₹13,500 crore contract signed with HAL for 12 Su-30MKIs A textbook example of how not to manage a procurement. OEM that forgot about upgrades, users who weren’t looking for updates (no one was bothered about upgrade) because Su57 was coming as FGFA.
tweets.content:
This is how Su30 fleet was created over the years :

📅 30 Nov 1996 – 40 aircraft (Russia)
📅 18 Dec 1998 – 10 aircraft (Russia)
📅 28 Dec 2000 – 140 aircraft (HAL)
📅 10 Oct 2007 – 40 aircraft (HAL)
📅 24 Dec 2012 – 42 aircraft (HAL)

After that, there was a gap of over a decade.

📅 15 Sep 2023 – AoN for 12 replacement aircraft
📅 12 Dec 2024 – ₹13,500 crore contract signed with HAL for 12 Su-30MKIs

A textbook example of how not to manage a procurement. OEM that forgot about upgrades, users who weren’t looking for updates (no one was bothered about upgrade) because Su57 was coming as FGFA.

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: 21/22 Great Nicobar Project A strategic infrastructure initiative on Great Nicobar Island aimed at strengthening India’s presence in the eastern Indian Ocean. • Enhances the Andaman & Nicobar Command as a tri-service base • Upgrades ports and airfields for faster troop and equipment movement • Improves maritime surveillance along key shipping routes near the Malacca Strait • Supports India’s forward defence posture in the Indo-Pacific Better connectivity, rapid deployment capability, and stronger maritime security for India.
tweets.content:
21/22

Great Nicobar Project

A strategic infrastructure initiative on Great Nicobar Island aimed at strengthening India’s presence in the eastern Indian Ocean.
• Enhances the Andaman & Nicobar Command as a tri-service base
• Upgrades ports and airfields for faster troop and equipment movement
• Improves maritime surveillance along key shipping routes near the Malacca Strait
• Supports India’s forward defence posture in the Indo-Pacific Better connectivity, rapid deployment capability, and stronger maritime security for India.

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: Officials from the Bangladesh High Commission are also present at the event. This is Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s first major public address in West Bengal after the formation of the BJP government in the state. The event is being held in the Tarakeswar area of Hooghly district, where a large number of people have gathered to witness the Prime Minister’s visit and the launch of several development initiatives. @indiatvnews

tweets.title: Allowing the Su-30MKI production line to go dormant was a strategic blunder. Fighter aircraft production is not a tap that can be turned on and off at will. Once orders dry up, suppliers move on, skilled workers are reassigned, and the industrial ecosystem begins to unravel.
tweets.content: Allowing the Su-30MKI production line to go dormant was a strategic blunder. Fighter aircraft production is not a tap that can be turned on and off at will. Once orders dry up, suppliers move on, skilled workers are reassigned, and the industrial ecosystem begins to unravel.
